title: Dormant Windows-encoding landmines: 6 test-file open() calls read prose/log files without encoding='utf-8'
current_state: T-275 fixed this exact bug in testing/test_modes.py:71/:98, and a same-day sibling fix covered testing/test_persistence.py:104 — both crashed with UnicodeDecodeError ('charmap' codec) once the file they read gained a multi-byte character past the cp1252 decode boundary. The remaining sites of the identical class, verified by grep 2026-07-07, are dormant only because their target files are currently ASCII-clean: testing/test_modes.py:26 (reads prompts/consciousness.txt — 400+ lines of prose, high multi-byte risk), testing/test_memory_roundtrip.py:61 (reads logs/evolution.jsonl), testing/test_system_introspect.py:73 (reads evolution log), testing/test_session_id_propagation.py:45 and :124 (read log files), testing/test_evolution_schema.py:142 (reads log file). Each is a plain open(path) with no encoding argument, so Windows defaults to cp1252 and any future non-ASCII content in those files breaks the suite.
target_state: All six sites pass encoding='utf-8'. A small AST-based guard test (testing/test_open_encoding.py, modeled exactly on testing/test_no_bare_except.py) fails if any open() call in testing/ reads a text file without an encoding argument, so the class cannot recur. verify.py PASS.
migration_plan: 1. Write testing/test_open_encoding.py: walk testing/*.py with ast, flag ast.Call nodes whose func is open with a mode that is absent/'r' and no encoding keyword; assert the offender list is exactly the 6 known sites (it will fail — that is the reproduction).
2. Add encoding='utf-8' to each of the six open() calls listed in current_state.
3. Flip the guard test to assert zero offenders.

4. python -m pytest testing/test_open_encoding.py testing/test_modes.py testing/test_memory_roundtrip.py testing/test_system_introspect.py testing/test_session_id_propagation.py testing/test_evolution_schema.py -v (note: test_memory_roundtrip is in COSTLY_TESTS — its collection-time syntax is still checked by verify.py; do not run its live API path).
5. python scripts/verify.py — must print PASS (never pipe it, T-214). SOLUTIONS.jsonl entry, close ticket, refresh_pi.py.
risk_notes: Deliberately NOT hand-fixed at filing time: this is the first genuine small, verified, SAFE-component (testing/-only) ticket since the queue emptied, i.e. exactly the candidate T-256 has been waiting for. Let scripts/sprint.py close it: rehearse with --dry-run --ticket T-276, then --auto-implement --ticket T-276 --max-cost 0.50, Ash gates the merge. If sprint.py fails on it, the run transcript is the deliverable and a hand-fix afterwards is fine. Two of the six files (test_memory_roundtrip.py) are COSTLY_TESTS — the edit is still safe because only the open() call changes, not the test's API usage.
